name: Prompt suggestions
problem: Users often do not know what an AI system can do or how to ask effectively.
solution: Show contextual example prompts that reveal capabilities and give users editable starting points.
problemContext: An AI feature accepts open-ended user input.
Users may not know the system's capabilities, limits, or useful request shape.
selectionRules: Choose prompt suggestions when users need help discovering what the AI can do.
Prefer a constrained form when the task has fixed required inputs and predictable outputs.
Avoid suggestions that imply capabilities, data access, or reliability the system does not have.
requiredStates: Selected state where a suggestion becomes editable input.
Submitted state after the user intentionally sends the prompt.
Unavailable or limited-capability state when suggestions should be suppressed.
interactionContract: Selecting a suggestion should populate or adapt the input, not silently perform high-impact work.
Suggestions should reflect current context and known capabilities.
Users must be able to edit suggested text before submission when consequences matter.
implementationChecklist: Make suggestions specific to the user's current task or object.
Keep suggestions truthful about system capability.
Place selected text in an editable input.
Avoid auto-submitting suggested prompts for consequential actions.
commonMisuses: Showing generic prompts unrelated to the current context.
Overpromising what the AI can do.
Treating suggestions as final answers rather than starting points.
critiqueQuestions: Would these suggestions help a user form a better request without misleading them about capability?
useWhen: The AI feature supports open-ended input.
Users need help forming a useful request.
avoidWhen: Suggestions would imply capabilities the system cannot reliably perform.
The task is constrained enough that a normal form is clearer.
accessibility: Suggestions should be reachable by keyboard and screen readers.
Selecting a suggestion should not submit without giving users a chance to edit when consequences matter.
keyboardBehavior: Arrow-key navigation may be appropriate for suggestion lists.
Tab order should not trap users inside suggestions.
failureModes: Overpromising what the AI can do.
Using generic suggestions unrelated to the user's context.
Making suggestions look like final answers.

sources.note: Human-AI guidelines emphasize making clear what the system can do and how well it can do it.
